description: Fix silent data loss in SDK storage utilities when window.localStorage is undefined. setLocalStorage and removeLocalStorage silently no-op without any warning when the host app deletes window.localStorage (e.g., Discord), while getLocalStorage and findLocalStorageEntry have an iframe fallback for the same scenario. Add the iframe fallback to write/remove operations and log a warning when the fallback is used.

userStories.title: Add iframe fallback and warning to setLocalStorage and removeLocalStorage
userStories.description: setLocalStorage and removeLocalStorage silently no-op when window.localStorage is undefined (e.g., Discord deletes the property). getLocalStorage already has an iframe fallback for this exact scenario. Add the same iframe fallback to the write and remove functions, and log a warning when the fallback is used so plugin developers have diagnostic visibility.
acceptanceCriteria: setLocalStorage successfully persists values when window.localStorage is undefined by using the same iframe fallback pattern as getLocalStorage
removeLocalStorage successfully removes keys when window.localStorage is undefined by using the same iframe fallback pattern
Both functions log a log.warn message when the iframe fallback is used (so plugin developers can diagnose the non-standard environment)
Both functions still return gracefully (no throw) when the iframe fallback also fails (e.g., sandboxed context where iframe creation throws)
Existing behavior is preserved: direct localStorage access is used when window.localStorage is defined, SecurityError/QuotaExceededError are caught and warned
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.ts (add iframe fallback to setLocalStorage and removeLocalStorage)
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.test.ts (add tests for the new fallback behavior)

Context:
The SDK runs in browser page context (injected as IIFEs). Some web apps like Discord delete `window.localStorage` from the global scope. The SDK already handles this for reads — `getLocalStorage` (lines 16-54) and `findLocalStorageEntry` (lines 62-103) both have an iframe fallback that creates a hidden same-origin iframe to access the underlying storage.

However, `setLocalStorage` (lines 109-116) and `removeLocalStorage` (lines 148-155) do NOT have this fallback. When `window.localStorage` is undefined, they silently no-op:

```typescript
export const setLocalStorage = (key: string, value: string): void => {
  try {
    const storage = window.localStorage as Storage | undefined;
    storage?.setItem(key, value);
  } catch (error) {
    log.warn(`setLocalStorage failed for key "${key}"`, error);
  }
};
```

When `storage` is `undefined`, `storage?.setItem(key, value)` evaluates to `undefined` (no-op). No catch triggers. No warning logs. The caller has no indication the write was dropped.

Changes:
1. Extract the iframe fallback pattern into a shared helper (e.g., `withIframeFallback`) to avoid duplicating the iframe creation/cleanup logic across 4 functions. The helper should:
   - Create a hidden iframe, append to document.body
   - Access iframe.contentWindow.localStorage
   - Execute a callback with the iframe's storage
   - Remove the iframe in a finally block
   - Return null if anything fails

2. Update `setLocalStorage` to use the iframe fallback when `window.localStorage` is undefined. Log a warning via `log.warn` when the fallback is used (e.g., `"setLocalStorage: localStorage unavailable, using iframe fallback for key 'X'"`)

3. Update `removeLocalStorage` with the same pattern.

4. Optionally refactor `getLocalStorage` and `findLocalStorageEntry` to use the shared helper too, reducing duplication. This is a nice-to-have — the primary goal is fixing the write/remove asymmetry.

5. Add tests mirroring the existing iframe fallback tests for getLocalStorage:
   - Test that setLocalStorage persists values when localStorage is undefined (mock iframe with working storage)
   - Test that removeLocalStorage removes keys when localStorage is undefined
   - Test that both functions return gracefully when iframe creation throws SecurityError
   - Test that a log.warn is emitted when the iframe fallback is used

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the function signatures or return types
- Do NOT change the behavior when window.localStorage IS defined (the normal path must be unchanged)
- The iframe fallback must clean up the iframe in a finally block (same pattern as getLocalStorage)
- The iframe must have `style.display = 'none'` (same as existing pattern)
- Errors in the iframe fallback must be caught silently (return gracefully, optionally log.warn) — never throw from these functions
- The existing tests must continue to pass unchanged

Trap warning:
Do NOT use `document.body.appendChild` without first checking that `document.body` exists. In very early page lifecycle, `document.body` may be null. The existing getLocalStorage iframe fallback does not check this, and neither needs to — the SDK runs after the adapter IIFE is injected, which happens after DOMContentLoaded. But if you add a guard, keep it consistent across all functions.
